channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Win a Championship Gaming Chair with Insider Gaming Esports
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming Esports is excited to partner with AutoFull to give one lucky reader the chance to win the chair that hosted the champions during the recent IEM Cologne tournament. On the stage in Germany, teams sat atop the AutoFull M6 Ultra 2.0, an ergonomic and advanced esports gaming chair with incredible features.

Below, you’ll learn how you can enter the giveaway and potentially win a stunning gaming chair that, out of sale, retails for a whopping $899.99. In our recent review, we wrote, ‘you’ll not find many chairs that offer the standard that the M6 Ultra 2.0 does.’

How to Enter The Giveaway for the AutoFull M6 Ultra 2.0 Chair

If you want to be in with a chance of winning the AutoFull M6 Ultra 2.0 chair with Insider Gaming Esports, you need to complete two simple steps.

First, you’ll need to follow Insider Gaming Esports on X.

Then, you’ll need to join the Insider Gaming Discord server and head to the ‘Giveaways’ channel to officially log your participation.

The entry window runs from July 1st to Monday, July 6th. When that window has closed, a winner will be automatically picked from the entrants on Discord, and the winner will be immediately notified. At that point, our friends at AutoFull will facilitate shipping a chair directly to the winner.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server what you think about the AutoFull M6 Ultra 2.0 chair, and make sure you complete both steps above to have your entry counted for this giveaway!

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the full Esports World Cup 2026 schedule, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming newsletter for the latest news available

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: CINDER CITY is The Division Meets Stellar Blade, With a PvE Twist
item.content:encoded:
CINDER CITY has just landed on Steam, at the same time as the developers at BigFire Games shared an in-depth preview of this upcoming open-world shooter. As one of these exciting games coming in under the radar, CINDER CITY might not have landed on your wishlist just yet, but it certainly has managed to find a spot on mine!

BigFire Games is a subsidiary of NC, the game’s publisher, and CINDER CITY is the team’s first game. It looks like an ambitious starting effort, taking elements from games like The Division and Stellar Blade and fusing them in a gritty, post-apocalyptic world.

Become a CINDERKNIGHT in CINDER CITY

CINDER CITY is set to burst into the MMORPG space at some point, bringing years of experience from NC’s teams and using them to flesh out a post-apocalyptic world in ‘near-future’ Seoul.

The Steam page that went live for CINDER CITY on June 30 paints the best picture possible of this game.

It’s a tactical third-person shooter that pits players against monstrous creatures in the fight to take back Seoul, which has been overrun. As players fight, either alone or with others, they’ll make use of vehicles, mech suits, and even aircraft ‘scattered across the open world.’

With a choice and consequence model at the heart of the game, players will pick their missions, take on dynamic events, and explore CINDER CITY how they see fit. And, as the game’s brief promises, ‘the choices you make will shape what this city becomes.’

  • RELATED: Learn How The Division Started Life

You’ve got all the trappings of a typical MMO in CINDER CITY, including dungeons, world raids, immense bosses, and a role-based system that leans into the traditional structure of the genre.

These elements sit nicely alongside gameplay that’s reminiscent of both The Division and Stellar Blade. The latter’s influence can be felt in some of the game’s hulking creatures, and all three games have a similar post-apocalyptic streak running through them.

Here’s the most recent preview for CINDER CITY:

Does CINDER CITY look like your kind of game? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new screenshots from the ‘Last of Us’ multiplayer game, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: When Will PS3 & PS Vita Online Stores Close? All Regions & Dates
item.content:encoded:
It’s a sad day for PlayStation fans, as it’s been revealed that the digital storefronts for the PlayStation 3 and PlayStation Vita are shutting down. Up until now, they’ve been online, with business running as usual for the older generation of consoles and handhelds, but the tough decision to turn them offline has been made.

As mentioned in the official statement, Sony notes that the call has been made due to updated payment processing standards. Unfortunately, the PS3 and PS Vita aren’t able to support these updates at the level required, leading to the closure of their associated PlayStation Stores.

That said, they’re not shutting down immediately, so you still have time to purchase and download digital titles before the stores go offline for good. With that in mind, here’s when the PS3 and PS Vita online stores close, across all regions.

When Will The PlayStation 3 Store Close?

The PlayStation 3 digital store will begin to close in August 2026 for select regions, though for most of the world, you’ll have until 2027 to make final purchases.

Specific dates haven’t been provided just yet, but PlayStation has given us months as windows for the closures. Latin America and the Middle East suffer first, with the digital store closing this year, whilst the rest of the world has until 2027.

  • More Insider Gaming: When Will PlayStation Stop Making Discs? PlayStation Store Changes, Explained

For the full PS3 store closure timeline, check out the table below:

When Will The PS Vita Store Close?

It’s the same story for the PlayStation Vita; the digital store will begin to close in August 2026, with final closures scheduled for July 2027.

There is a great selection of handheld games available and exclusive to the PS Vita. If you still have one collecting dust at home, or never got your hands on the handheld, now might be the perfect opportunity, as you’re on the clock to make some final digital purchases from its PlayStation Store.

  • More Insider Gaming: PlayStation’s No Disc Plans for 2028 Could Hint at PS6 Release Date

For the full PS Vita store closure timeline, check out the table below:

Losing a digital store is always a sad moment for gamers, but everything has to come to an end eventually. Still, what an era that generation of console was…

Will you be making any final purchases for the PS3 or PS Vita? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Review Embargo Date, and be sure to sign up for our free Weekly Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: When Will PlayStation Stop Making Discs? PlayStation Store Changes, Explained
item.content:encoded:
In a recent update, the team at Sony Interactive Entertainment revealed a drastic shift coming for the world of PlayStation. In a paradigm change, PlayStation is moving away from physical discs to an all-digital landscape, ceasing the production of discs in response to ‘shifting trends in consumer preference.’

This is a massive overhaul to the gaming space that meets the increasingly digital nature of the business head-on. It’s a fairly obvious move, but it’s sure to rub some gamers the very wrong way.

Read on to learn when PlayStation discs will stop being made and what this means for you as a gamer.

Will All Sony PlayStation Games Be Digital?

From January 2028, all new PlayStation games will be digital only, Sony confirmed in a blog post published on July 1, 2026. This means that any games released on or after January 2028 will not have a traditional disc.

Instead, publishers and retailers will have to rely on codes in a box or on the digital storefronts that have quickly become the number-one destination to buy new games.

In the blog post, the team explained:

This is a natural direction for Sony Interactive Entertainment to adapt to consumer trends as the general preference for digital media significantly outpaces physical discs. This transition will enable us to align more closely with how most of our community prefers to access and play games today.

This move does not impact any games released prior to January 2028, so don’t worry. It’s not like you’ll wake up on January 1, 2028, and be met with an inability to play discs in your PlayStation 5 console.

How Does This Change Impact Me as a Gamer?

The most critical way this impacts you as a gamer is in your purchasing habits. This will effectively eradicate most game sharing (between friends), second-hand, and physical sale markets going forward. There will still be a back catalogue, but it’ll eventually fade away over the following years.

If you’ve always loved physical media such as discs, you’ll be taking a knock when this change lands in January 2028. Every new PlayStation game you buy beyond that point will be 100% digital.

  • RELATED: No Disc Plans Hint at PS6 Release Date

It’s a safe bet that the PlayStation 6 will launch without a physical drive, but owners should have the option to bolt one on for backwards compatibility purposes, assuming the PS6 accommodates that function.

Additionally, Sony has announced the impending closure of digital stores for the PlayStation 3 and PS Vita platforms. By July 2027, both of these digital storefronts will be closed globally, bringing an end to twenty years of gaming history.

What do you think about this update? Are you mad at PlayStation for scrapping the production of discs? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that NBCUniversal might get into video games, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: PlayStation’s No Disc Plans for 2028 Could Hint at PS6 Release Date
item.content:encoded:
A new PlayStation announcement to stop discs by 2028 may have given players a hint about PlayStation 6’s release date.

Players have been hearing a lot of new reports about PlayStation’s next-gen console recently, with reports that the hardware will not be sold at significant losses, while $1000 rumors persist. Now, a new announcement from PlayStation is leading players to believe the next console might not arrive until 2028.

PS6 Won’t Arrive Until 2028 At the Earliest, Analysts Predict

For those who may not be aware, the latest announcement indicates that Sony will stop producing physical discs for new PlayStation games in 2028. Following this news, speculation about the PS6’s release date has begun. A report by Game File reveals that Piers Harding-Rolls, a senior games analyst at Ampere Analysis, has stated, “This pretty much guarantees that the PS6 won’t arrive until 2028 at the earliest.” Furthermore, the news also suggests that the base version of the PS6 may not include a disc drive.

  • More Insider Gaming: 13 Sony PlayStation Franchises We Need Back on PS5 & PS6

There are many questions regarding the upcoming console, primarily concerning backward compatibility and how players will be able to use game discs from the PS5 and PS4 on the PS6. However, Sony has not provided any information about the next-gen console; they have only announced their plans to stop producing discs. At the moment, players will have to wait for official confirmation from Sony about the PS6 release date and disc plans to know for sure.

What are your thoughts on the PS6 release date being revealed through Sony’s no-disc plans? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Arkane being at risk of closure with Blade possibly being canceled. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sony Closing Select PlayStation Stores in 2026 & 2027
item.content:encoded:
The PlayStation Store has long been Sony’s core digital front for games and associated media. It’s transcended several generations of PlayStation consoles and is the backbone of PS5 digital media, but some of the more archaic shops are about to be closed down.

Following the shocking news that Sony will stop producing discs for new PlayStation Games in 2028, we’ve now learned that some of the brand’s PS Stores are about to be shut down for good.

Sony Says Goodbye to PS3 and PS Vita PlayStation Stores

Here’s the full statement, courtesy of a July 1 Sony PlayStation blog:

‘After nearly two decades of supporting the PS3 console generation, we wanted to let you know we will be closing the PlayStation Store on PS3, as well as on PS Vita. PlayStation Store on PS3 will close in select markets starting this year, followed by global closures for PS3 and PS Vita next year. That means new content purchases will no longer be possible once the PlayStation Store closes on these devices. To ease the transition, players will still be able to download previously purchased content after the closing date for the foreseeable future. 

As the PlayStation Store continues to evolve to support modern commerce systems, including updated payment processing standards, PS3 and PS Vita are no longer able to support these updates at the level required. As a result, we will need to close PlayStation Store on these devices in the timeframes as follows:

In all other countries, PlayStation Store on PS3 and PS Vita will close in July 2027.

Mexico, Honduras, Nicaragua – PlayStation Store on PS3 will close starting August 2026.

Additional Latin American and Middle Eastern countries – PlayStation Store on PS3 will close starting late 2026.’

It appears the PlayStation 4 storefront will remain, which makes sense as the old-gen hardware continues to receive new releases—although they are lessening as the years go by.

  • More Insider Gaming: PlayStation 5 Update 26.04-13.42.00 Patch Notes

However, if you still enjoy your PS3 or PS Vita, you won’t be able to make any new purchases from either store, but older purchases will be available.

With news of a PS6 handheld that has a GPU more powerful than an Xbox Series S, and the inevitable arrival of the PS6, it makes sense for Sony to move forward.

What do you make of recent PlayStation changes? Let us know via the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about XBOX reminding gamers that Modern Warfare 4 isn’t coming to Game Pass yet, and be sure to sign up for our free Weekly Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton
